Home / Services / Walk-In Shower Conversion

Walk-In Shower Conversion

Transform outdated tubs into accessible, luxurious showers with proper waterproofing.

Converting tubs to walk-in showers requires particular attention to waterproofing in our freeze-thaw climate. We install multiple layers of protection: rubberized membranes beneath tile, sloped mortar beds, and epoxy grout resistant to temperature fluctuations.

For Victorian homes in Cambridge, we design period-appropriate subway tile showers with nickel fixtures. In Newton's Colonials, we often incorporate marble thresholds and built-in seating. Our frameless glass enclosures maintain sightlines while containing steam effectively, with hardware that withstands Boston's mineral-rich water.

Every conversion includes grab bar reinforcement (whether visible or behind walls) and proper pitch toward linear drains - critical details many contractors overlook in our service area.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Structural assessment of existing alcove walls and floor framing
  2. 02 Step 2: Custom shower pan construction with precise 1/4" per foot slope
  3. 03 Step 3: Multiple waterproofing layers tested before tile installation
  4. 04 Step 4: Final inspection of all seals and caulk joints

Benefits

  • Eliminates dangerous tub-step transitions
  • Reduces humidity with properly vented designs
  • Increases accessibility without clinical appearance
  • Prevents leaks that can damage historic wood framing

Frequently Asked Questions

Do walk-in showers make bathrooms feel colder?

We install radiant floor heating systems in most conversions, along with strategically placed towel warmers for cozy comfort during Boston winters.

How do you prevent glass shower doors from spotting?

We use commercial-grade glass with permanent hydrophobic coatings and recommend our maintenance-free water softening systems.

Get a Free Estimate

Ready to get started with walk-in shower conversion? Contact us today.
